title: Sentencing Act 2020 Schedule 21
text:
Title: Sentencing Act 2020 Schedule 21
Citation: Sentencing Act 2020, Schedule 21
Jurisdiction: uk
Source URL: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2020/17/schedule/21

Schedule 21 is titled Determination of minimum term in relation to mandatory life sentence for murder etc.

Paragraph 2 provides that if the court considers that the seriousness of the offence, or the combination of the offence and one or more offences associated with it, is exceptionally high, and the offender was aged 21 or over when the offence was committed, the appropriate starting point is a whole life order.

Schedule 21 sets statutory starting points for determining the minimum term in relation to a mandatory life sentence for murder, including whole life order starting points and term-of-years starting points depending on age and offence seriousness.
importance_reason: primary UK statute for murder sentencing starting points
